news.title: National flex workspace brands may command 25% pricing premium by FY28: Report
news.para: National coworking brands currently command a 19% pricing premium over independent operators, according to a report by myHQ. The report analysed 1,384 coworking centres across 30 cities based on five years of transaction data from more than 10,000 companies.

news.title: Telangana HC declines to halt sales in high value 400-acre Kukatpally land dispute
news.para: Telangana High Court refused to halt the sale of over 400 acres in Kukatpally, linked to GOCL Corporation and other private entities. While transactions will be subject to the final court decision, the court has directed the state government and concerned departments to file their responses within three weeks. The case involves allegations of irregularities in transferring government land to private hands, potentially causing significant revenue loss to the state.

news.title: NGT orders compensation assessment, restoration of lands affected by illegal brick kilns in NTR district
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has ordered NTR district authorities to assess and quantify environmental damage from illegal brick kilns within three months. The directive includes restoring agricultural lands and water bodies. The tribunal also mandated the AP Pollution Control Board to levy compensation on operators for air quality deterioration and soil fertility loss, emphasizing strict regulation of such activities.

news.title: Nine of 10 buildings fail fire safety checks across Nagpur's fire stations
news.para: Nagpur's fire safety compliance is alarmingly low, with over 90% of inspected buildings in some areas violating norms. RTI data reveals widespread non-compliance across multiple fire station jurisdictions, despite regular inspections. While the fire department efficiently processes No Objection Certificates and reports no fire-related deaths, the high violation rate highlights significant enforcement gaps. This stark contrast demands immediate attention to ensure public safety.

news.title: Telangana government forms committee to probe Dharani, Bhu Bharathi irregularities
news.para: The Telangana government has formed a special committee to investigate irregularities in the Dharani and Bhu Bharathi land portals. Following the identification of security flaws and unauthorized entries, the committee will identify those responsible, take stringent action, and propose preventive measures. The National Informatics Centre has been tasked with developing a new, secure system architecture.

news.title: Panchkula collects just ₹5.9 crore of target ₹100 crore property tax
news.para: Panchkula MC has collected a mere ₹5.9 crore from over ₹100 crore in outstanding property tax, even after a 100% interest waiver. Major defaulters, owing ₹40 crore, are now facing stricter measures. The municipal commissioner has ordered priority identification and issuance of rules-based notices, with legal action and property sealing as potential next steps to bolster the corporation's finances and civic service delivery.

news.title: Patna civic body collects nearly property tax worth ₹48 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Patna Municipal Corporation witnessed a record single-day property tax collection exceeding Rs3 crore on June 30, driven by a last-minute rush for a 5% rebate. This surge contributed to a strong first quarter, with total collections reaching nearly Rs48 crore, a significant increase from the previous year. Despite some technical glitches and long queues, taxpayers actively participated, reflecting improved compliance and higher rates.

news.title: Delhi HC refers plea to do away with iron grilles in buildings to MCD for action
news.para: Delhi High Court has directed the Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) to consider a pending representation regarding hazardous cage-like iron grilles and illegal projections in residential buildings. The court deemed a Public Interest Litigation seeking their removal too broad.  The petitioner highlighted recent fire tragedies, like the Vivek Vihar incident, where blocked escape routes exacerbated loss of life, emphasizing the urgent need for safety improvements.

news.title: Kreeva to invest over ₹1,050 crore in housing project in South Delhi
news.para: Kreeva, the real estate venture of Kanodia Group, will develop a luxury residential project near New Friends Colony in South Delhi. The project is backed by ₹260 crore investment from ASK Property Fund and will comprise 114 units.

news.title: Over 270 Bengaluru development body's sites to soon get relief; 55 still in limbo
news.para: Over 250 site owners in Banashankari 6th Phase, Bengaluru, are set to gain relief from a long-standing Turahalli forest buffer zone dispute. The Bangalore Development Authority (BDA) has proposed a one-time settlement with the forest department, aiming to grant full possession of legally allotted sites. This move follows years of uncertainty, with surveys narrowing down the affected properties.

news.title: Ahmedabad civic body looks to monetise over 209 properties
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is launching an ambitious asset monetisation drive, targeting over 200 civic properties including gardens, schools, and community halls.  A consultant has been appointed to explore revenue generation through amusement parks, food courts, and private management of facilities.  The initiative aims to unlock the value of properties estimated at over Rs 2,000 crore, with plans to rent out school buildings and allow private buses on BRTS corridors.

news.title: Bengaluru development body seeks to ease registration of apartment associations
news.para: Apartment owners in Bengaluru are pushing for greater control over their complexes. The Bengaluru Development Authority (BDA) is now urging the state government to designate a "competent authority" to register apartment owners' associations under the Karnataka Apartment Ownership Act. This move aims to resolve registration hurdles, grant legal recognition to associations, and streamline maintenance management, potentially easing resident grievances and ensuring RERA compliance.

news.title: Tamil Nadu housing minister pushes online sale of houses and plots
news.para: Tamil Nadu Housing Board is set to go fully digital, with all services and property sales moving online. Housing Minister B Rajkumar has directed officials to digitize operations for enhanced transparency and public access. This move aims to streamline the purchase of unsold housing units and plots, while also expediting new schemes and ongoing construction projects across the state.

news.title: BSE launches REITs and Commercial Real Estate Index for passive investment products
news.para: BSE Index Services has launched the BSE REITs and Commercial Real Estate Index, India's first benchmark tracking listed REITs and companies with significant commercial real estate exposure. The index is designed to support passive investment products such as ETFs and index funds while offering diversified exposure to yield-generating real estate assets.

news.title: Delhi government sets October 31 deadline to apply for ownership rights in unauthorised colonies
news.para: Delhi government has set October 31, 2026, as the deadline for property owners in unauthorized colonies to apply for conveyance deeds and authorization slips under the PM-UDAY Scheme. This move aims to boost applications, which have been low despite the regularization of 1,511 colonies. Residents are encouraged to apply to secure their property rights and regularize their homes.

news.title: Cement companies’ profitability likely to decline in Q1 FY27: Ind-Ra
news.para: India Ratings and Research expects cement companies’ profitability to decline in Q1 FY27 as price hikes may not fully offset higher fuel, freight and packing material costs. The agency expects cement demand to grow in mid-single digits during the quarter.

news.title: India retail leasing rises 18% to 2.4 million sq ft in Q2 2026: Report
news.para: Retail leasing across the top eight cities stood at 2.4 million sq ft in Q2 2026, up 17.6% year-on-year, according to Cushman & Wakefield. Delhi-NCR, Mumbai and Hyderabad together accounted for 64% of total leasing during the quarter.

news.title: Goa cabinet clears 'Mhajo Flat' scheme to grant ownership rights to flat owners
news.para: Goa's cabinet has greenlit the 'Mhajo Flat' scheme, offering ownership rights to thousands of flat owners facing title issues due to builder-landowner disputes or abandoned projects. This initiative, requiring a nominal fee and stamp duty, empowers flat owners to form cooperative societies for land conveyance, facilitating redevelopment of older buildings. Amendments to key acts aim to resolve long-standing ownership complexities and unlock redevelopment potential across the state.
